summary: Excellent audience recording of a high‑energy 6/30/86 Cincinnati show – great sound and many standout performances (Ship of Fools, Bertha, Smokestack, Loser, Cassidy).
review: The audience tape captures the band in top form; Jerry’s “smoking” guitar work and Bob’s vocal fire power the set while Phil adds a dark, percussive bass. Highlights repeatedly mentioned are Ship of Fools, Bertha/Women, Smokestack Lightning, Loser, Cassidy and the Good Lovin’/Mighty Quinn closer, all delivered with an upbeat, groovy vibe. Recording quality is praised as clear and well‑balanced for an audience source, making the show highly enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly positive about both performance and sound; strong song highlights and consistent praise for recording clarity outweigh a few mixed comments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the tape as “great sound”, “excellent audience recording” and note it is clear enough to hear vocals and instruments, with only occasional remarks of average quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans repeatedly note a groovy, upbeat flow – a tight first set followed by an even tighter second set that builds energy and ends with a memorable Good Lovin'/Mighty Quinn closer.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for “smoking” guitar tone, fluid interplay with Bob, and consistent strong playing throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as sounding “dark”, with occasional bass that blends into the rhythm section; one reviewer noted he was hard to hear but still solid.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as outstanding; his stage presence (lights on Weir) and interplay with Jerry highlighted, though a few critics mentioned overly dramatic blues attempts.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards described as reverbed and distant by one reviewer, while another praised Brent’s tone and how it fit the venue acoustics perfectly.
band_member_comments.Drums: Percussion noted for African‑like touches, matching rain sounds in the second set, and an inventive drum/space section that stood out.
